completed_slices.summary: Implemented latest active run lookup scoped by repository path, exposed it through daemon status as optional RunDetails, and added CLI support for khazad-doom status --repo <path> --latest returning null when no active run matches.
completed_slices.commit_message: Add latest active run status lookup
public_interfaces_changed: Added StatusParams fields: repo_path, latest, active_only
acceptance_status: State latest-run query is deterministic, repo-scoped, and supports active-only pending/running filtering.
Daemon status latest lookup returns the existing RunDetails shape with progress and events when a run matches.
CLI status --repo <path> --latest prints RunDetails JSON or null when no active run matches.
Existing status --run and daemon-wide status paths remain covered by integration tests.
Added unit/integration coverage for latest active selection, repo scoping, and empty-result behavior.
assumptions: CLI --latest is monitor-oriented and therefore uses active-only lookup; daemon IPC can request non-active latest by setting active_only=false.

completed_slices.summary: Added a harness-neutral `monitor` command with run-specific follow mode, latest-active repo mode, compact dashboard rendering, and `--once` snapshots. Updated event retrieval to return recent events and added integration coverage.
public_interfaces_changed: Run status events now return the latest bounded events in chronological order
acceptance_status: Implemented latest repo monitor wait/attach loop with waiting snapshots and future-run continuation.
Implemented specific run monitor follow mode with success only on completed and errors for failed/blocked/cancelled/interrupted.
Dashboard shows run id/status, phase, slice, command, elapsed, message, recent events, and bounded output tail.
Implemented --once snapshot mode.
Preserved watch and status --follow fallback commands.
assumptions: Harness-neutral means terminal/stdout rendering only; no OS windows, tmux panes, or external terminals were opened.

completed_slices.summary: Documented the always-on monitor UX, updated the Khazad-Doom skill to prefer monitor --latest over blocking --wait for Pi runs, and extended khazad-doom run CLI JSON with repo and monitor commands.
public_interfaces_changed: khazad-doom run JSON now includes repo_path, monitor_command, and run_monitor_command
tests_run: manual smoke: target/debug/khazad-doom run --repo <temp-repo> --agent fake --all JSON monitor fields
acceptance_status: README explains TUI as terminal user interface and recommends khazad-doom monitor --repo . --latest.
README distinguishes watch as plain fallback, monitor as dashboard, and Pi extension as optional adapter, not core workflow state.
Skill recommends monitor --latest / emitted monitor_command and warns not to rely on blocking --wait for real Pi runs.
khazad-doom run JSON includes repo_path, monitor_command, and run_monitor_command.
Docs explicitly say Khazad-Doom does not auto-open external windows by default.
assumptions: Interpreted khazad-doom run JSON as the CLI output for khazad-doom run.

completed_slices.summary: Added a Pi package manifest and optional /khazad-monitor Pi TUI overlay extension that polls khazad-doom status JSON, renders run/progress/events/output tail, degrades cleanly outside TUI, and documents installation/use.
public_interfaces_changed: Pi package manifest declares skills and extensions
acceptance_status: complete: package.json declares the existing skill and optional khazad-monitor extension without changing global settings.
complete: /khazad-monitor supports latest and run-id modes and renders status JSON fields in a Pi TUI overlay.
complete: q and Esc close only the overlay; no cancel command is issued.
complete: non-TUI and unavailable-khazad cases show fallback commands or overlay errors without stack traces.
complete: extension syntax check passes and README/skill docs explain optional installation and use.
assumptions: The khazad-doom binary is available on PATH or via KHAZAD_DOOM_BIN for the extension.
Users explicitly install or enable the Pi package; this slice does not modify user Pi settings.

integration_repair.summary: Normalized CLI repo path handling to the git repository root so run JSON monitor commands and status/monitor --latest work when invoked from subdirectories. Added regression coverage and left the worktree clean.
tests_run: manual subdir monitor smoke using target/debug/khazad-doom with temp HOME/repo

findings.description: run --repo <subdir> emitted a monitor_command scoped to the subdirectory while the daemon stored runs under the git root, causing status/monitor --repo <subdir> --latest to return null. The CLI now normalizes repo paths to the git root.
